qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default text-format parser recursion limit allows stack-exhaustion DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes C++ TextFormat::Parser recursion in src/google/protobuf/text_format.cc and text_format.h, while the advisory patches PHP php/src/Google/Protobuf/Internal/CodedInputStream.php for negative varint length handling and recursion-budget enforcement. The repository and general DoS theme overlap, but the vulnerable component and root cause do not.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's PHP parser bugs. The only qualifying finding is a different C++ text-format parser stack-exhaustion issue.
evidence: The advisory summary and details scope the issue to the Protobuf PHP library parsing untrusted input with negative varints or deep recursion.
Commit 60e93d2d104f2af9cd345b1c6f3891d91430244a patches php/src/Google/Protobuf/Internal/CodedInputStream.php so readRaw rejects negative size values read from the wire.
Commit c8e9b27d95c6ab2d0668b5889e7dac2c477b7038 patches php/src/Google/Protobuf/Internal/CodedInputStream.php so recursion_budget, not recursion_limit, is decremented and enforced.

The qualifying finding instead cites src/google/protobuf/text_format.cc and src/google/protobuf/text_format.h, with an exploit path through C++ TextFormat::Parser recursion_limit_=INT_MAX on text-format input.
